collection_mode: manual review of visible search results and four fixed public post pages; no engagement actions
scope: Second-wave validation of candidate mechanism terms and nightlife slang discovered in the first Xiaohongshu pass.

queries.observation: The visible results were overwhelmingly mahjong or card-game content, with no stable party-game use observed.
No exact combined use was visible. The page fell back to generic items about 局头, 局头备忘录, and 抓手指.
The visible results were almost entirely Chinese audio-drama or radio-drama content rather than a party-game term.
A dense cluster of training, icebreaker, team-building, dinner-table, and host-led party results used the same named reaction-game family.
Nearly every visible result used A仔 in a nightlife or bar-grouping context. This is a role and market-slang entry, not a game-mechanism entry.
The visible results concerned game skins or weapon trading; no party-game meaning was observed.
No exact 收枪版 variant appeared, but the query returned multiple broader 抓手指 tutorials, origin stories, host notes, complete-version claims, and atmosphere-led variants.
All seven images in the fixed post were reviewed manually. The compilation mixes reusable trigger structures with many alcohol, contact, privacy, smoking, fire, and physical-safety risks.

fixed_sources.title: 培训相关 | 破冰小游戏01 逢三抓手
fixed_sources.paraphrased_observation: Players form a circle and prepare one hand to catch a neighbor while keeping the other hand ready to escape. A narrated target-digit cue triggers the simultaneous reaction. Multiple rounds can add false cues or pacing changes.
fixed_sources.productization: Suitable as an alcohol-free reflex game when the target cue, hand placement, and opt-out are explained before play.

fixed_sources.title: 夜店酒吧黑话
fixed_sources.paraphrased_observation: The post groups organizer, recruiting, ordinary-participant, price, eligibility, table-composition, and appearance-gating vocabulary. It is evidence for a nightlife market lexicon, not for a party-game rule set.

fixed_sources.title: 酒吧小白科普
fixed_sources.paraphrased_observation: A second explainer independently places 局头, management or recruiting roles, A仔, male/female free-entry labels, and venue sales in a role hierarchy. This corroborates the role cluster without making every definition universal.

fixed_sources.title: 2026年 还有人去酒吧不知道怎么玩！
fixed_sources.paraphrased_observation: The seven-image compilation contains a large set of trigger ideas. Only reusable mechanism classes and risk categories were retained; individual unsafe prompts were not transcribed.

slang_evidence.working_interpretation: In these two posts, an ordinary male participant who pays or shares the table cost, rather than the organizer or host.
slang_evidence.caveat: Nightlife usage can vary by city, venue, and creator. Treat this as a working interpretation, not a universal dictionary definition.

slang_evidence.working_interpretation: Presented as a female counterpart within the same participant and payment vocabulary.
slang_evidence.caveat: Requires more creator-level and city-level corroboration before formal normalization.

slang_evidence.working_interpretation: One commenter described a transfer between organizers when a participant group could not be assembled.
slang_evidence.caveat: Do not promote this to a stable definition without repeated independent use.

slang_evidence.working_interpretation: One commenter treated it as price-related shorthand involving six units received.
slang_evidence.caveat: The unit and scope are unclear; retain only as an unverified search lead.

safe_mechanism_classes.description: A spoken target cue triggers catch-and-escape or another low-contact reflex action.
safe_mechanism_classes.guard: Explain hand placement and allow a no-contact gesture substitute.

safe_mechanism_classes.description: A displayed instruction refers to the act of viewing the screen itself, creating a harmless reveal or point trigger.
safe_mechanism_classes.guard: Use points or a playful task rather than drinking.

safe_mechanism_classes.description: A host-provided calculator, app screen, or fictional device state becomes the trigger.
safe_mechanism_classes.guard: Never require participants to expose private apps, messages, galleries, contacts, or location.

safe_mechanism_classes.description: A beat or gesture sequence determines when players act.
safe_mechanism_classes.guard: Keep movements simple, seated-friendly, and skippable.

safe_mechanism_classes.description: Use a safe object pass, secret-hand guess, or action chain to build timing and memory.
safe_mechanism_classes.guard: No mouth transfer, fragile objects, forced touch, or unsafe speed.

safe_mechanism_classes.description: A die, card, or coin selects a player, category, or harmless task.
safe_mechanism_classes.guard: No money, alcohol quantity, humiliation, or non-consensual assignment.

separation_rule.gameplay_river: Only named mechanisms, reusable trigger structures, and safe adaptations enter the gameplay collection.
separation_rule.risk_slang_river: Recruiting, price, eligibility, appearance-gating, and participant-market slang stays in a separate risk lexicon and is never turned into a game mechanic.

constraints: No screenshots, authentication tokens, session query parameters, private account data, or long copied captions are stored.
Visible-card counts are manual lower-bound snapshots rather than platform-wide prevalence estimates.
Single-comment explanations are retained only as low-confidence search leads.
Unsafe rules from the seven-image compilation are represented only as risk categories and safe mechanism classes.
